TextENGNew: The next day, at lunchtime.
The whole class was buzzing with whatever rumors they pleased about Rena's whereabouts.
changeReason: Old translation says 'disappearance' (消息 actually means 'whereabouts/news'); new correctly renders it as 'rumors about Rena's whereabouts,' changing the implication from confirmed vanishing to uncertain speculation.

TextENGNew: My friends were all worried about Rena's safety, but... I alone kept my distance.
changeReason: Old translation includes narrator in the group ('We were all worried'); new correctly separates him ('My friends were all worried... I alone kept my distance'), matching the JPN 仲間たち (friends/companions).

TextENGNew: ``...I wonder where Rena went.``
``Some people are saying she was spirited away by the demons.@k@vS09/03/170300489. Honestly!``
``Keiichi-san, you seem to be lacking your usual energy today...``
Satoko and Rika-chan noticed something was off about me and came over to talk.
Ever since Rena's words last night... I hadn't been able to sleep at all.
Keeping secrets means you're not really friends.
That single line had lodged itself in me like a thorn... and the pain of it had kept me awake.
``Mion.@k@vS09/01/170100957. ...Satoko too, and Rika-chan.@k@vS09/01/170100958. ...There's something I need to tell you.``
``...Is this about Rena?``
``No... it's not.@k@vS09/01/170100960. It's about me...``
I believed they were my friends, so I decided to tell them.
...But what would actually change by telling them?
They would only look down on me for it... What good would it do me?
...None.
I simply... wanted to heal the pain of Rena's words. That was all.
``About... yourself, Keiichi-san...?``
changeReason: Old translation is a general 'What is this about?'; new correctly captures Satoko asking specifically about Keiichi himself ('About... yourself, Keiichi-san?'), matching JPN 圭一さんのこと.

TextENGNew: ``...Yeah. This is serious.@k@vS09/01/170100962. Please hear me out.``
changeReason: Old translates 真面目な話 as 'the truth about myself' but it means 'a serious matter'; new correctly says 'This is serious.'

TextENGNew: ``It's about... what happened before I moved here.@k@vS09/01/170100964. Back then, I was... the kind of kid who had nothing going for him except studying.``

At cram school, I learned the tricks to the game called studying.@k I discovered the thrill of basking in superiority through good grades, and kept studying for the utterly trivial reason of wanting to look down on the rest of the class.
When my grades went up, my parents were delighted and my teachers fawned over me.@k It felt good.
The kids who strutted around showing off at the fifty-meter dash, or dodgeball, or swimming—they all looked so small to me, and that feeling alone was enough reason to keep studying.
Before I knew it, my scores were competing not just for the top of my class, but for the top of the entire school.
changeReason: Old says he 'was the top' definitively; new correctly says 'competing for the top' (トップを争う = competing/vying for the top), a meaningful distinction about his academic standing.

TextENGNew: So I came to believe that I was the most important person in the school.
I was the kind of worthless kid who liked to look down from the window at the other students playing in the schoolyard during lunch, savoring the feeling of superiority.
...As my grades climbed, the expectations around me grew to match.
Suddenly everyone was aiming impossibly high, taking it for granted that I would enroll at some elite school with a ludicrously high entrance threshold.
And of course, I was conceited enough to believe I deserved to get in.
But... even though the burden of studying hadn't changed at all, the pleasure of looking down on others kept fading.
The people around me seemed to feel the same—my classmates stopped praising my grades, and my teachers only pushed me to aim higher.
I was rapidly losing any joy in studying.
I had never liked studying in the first place.
I was just an unremarkable brat with average athleticism and average toughness, who had stumbled upon the one thing that let him feel superior to others and latched onto it.
If unicycling or kendama had given me that same feeling of superiority, I would have practiced those instead.
changeReason: Old translates けん玉 (kendama) as 'juggling'; new correctly preserves 'kendama,' a specific traditional Japanese toy distinct from juggling.

TextENGNew: Once I started to recognize those feelings for what they were, studying became nothing but tedium.
That's right—I was just a spoiled kid who wanted someone to praise him whenever he did something impressive.
...So as long as I got praised, I'd finish my summer homework on time and volunteer to clean the classroom.
And when the praise stopped coming, my entire life felt meaningless.
There was a time when my allowance was proportional to my grades, so I had more spending money than any kid my age should have had.
...Whether I'd always had a taste for it, or whether my pent-up emotions drove me to seek out violence, I don't know.
I started taking an interest in model guns.
changeReason: Old translates モデルガン as 'BB guns'; new correctly says 'model guns.' These are different types of replica weapons.

TextENGNew: Shooting at cardboard boxes was harmless enough.
As long as I was taking my aggression out on cardboard, it was just stress relief.@k Nobody would blame me for that.
But soon, I started wanting to do what I knew I shouldn't.
The warning label printed on the model gun box read,@k 'Do not shoot at people.'
changeReason: Old adds 'That was when I thought about doing just that' which is not in JPN. The JPN only states what the warning label said. New is more faithful.

TextENGNew: Why did I deliberately do the one thing I was told not to?@k That was the extent of my immaturity.
I had no interest in studying anymore, yet my parents kept forcing me to attend cram schools. I resented them for it.
I should have just told my parents that I didn't want to study anymore.
...But I was too small, too cowardly to find that courage.
...Of all things, my aggression turned toward complete strangers.
changeReason: Old specifies 'shot at kids who were younger' and adds detail about it being an excuse; JPN 見ず知らずの人 just says 'complete strangers.' New is more faithful to the JPN.

TextENGNew: Of course, I had no personal grudge against any of the people I targeted.
I had absolutely no intention of injuring anyone.
I would snipe them from a distance -- one or two shots -- and that alone was enough to satisfy me.
I'm studying hard. I'm doing something good.
So it's fine if I do something equally bad to balance it out.
With that unbelievably self-centered reasoning, I absolved myself.
...How selfish, how arrogant, how pathetically childish!
Even now, just laying it all out like this makes my skin crawl.
If I could go back and stand in front of my past self, I'd have punched him so hard his back teeth would fly out.
But at the time, I was completely absorbed in this game.
It never occurred to me that I'd get caught someday. I hadn't set any point at which I'd stop.
Then, one day.
...I spotted a passerby on a dark, narrow, deserted street, just like always.@k It was someone I'd never seen before.
changeReason: Old specifies 'a little girl' but Japanese 通行人 means 'passerby' without specifying gender or age.

TextENGNew: Maybe they were on their way somewhere, or running an errand.@k ...Either way, it didn't matter to me.
changeReason: Old uses 'she' (female) and 'friend's house'; JPN uses gender-neutral terms (どこかへ出掛ける途中). New uses 'they' preserving the JPN ambiguity.

TextENGNew: Despite all the commotion this had been causing in the neighborhood, how careless of them.
changeReason: Old adds 'The school had warned her not to' which is not in the JPN. JPN says 近所でも騒ぎになっている (causing commotion in the neighborhood). New is more faithful.

TextENGNew: Getting hit was their own fault.
changeReason: Old uses 'her'; JPN uses gender-neutral 被害. New uses 'their' preserving ambiguity.

TextENGNew: With that kind of selfish logic, I did what I always did... and shot.
Usually, the person would cry out in pain and run away.
changeReason: Old uses 'she cried'; new uses 'the person would cry.' JPN uses gender-neutral language throughout this passage.

TextENGNew: ...But this person's reaction was unlike anything I'd seen before.
changeReason: Old says 'this girl'; JPN says その人 (that person), gender-neutral. New says 'this person.'

TextENGNew: The way this person writhed in pain was beyond anything normal,@k and it was in that moment that I realized for the first time@k what a terrible thing I had been doing.
changeReason: Old says 'The girl howled'; JPN says その人の痛がり方 (that person's way of being in pain). New preserves gender neutrality.

TextENGNew: They had seen me.
changeReason: Old says 'She saw me'; JPN 相手 is gender-neutral. New says 'They had seen me.'

TextENGNew: So I couldn't be the one to take them to the hospital.
changeReason: Old says 'take her'; new says 'take them.' JPN is gender-neutral.

TextENGNew: In the end, I couldn't do anything... so I left them there, rolling on the ground in agony, and ran away.
changeReason: Old says 'left her there while she was still screaming'; new says 'left them there, rolling on the ground.' JPN says 転げ回る (rolling around), which new captures and old misses.

TextENGNew: Is that person all right?
changeReason: Old adds 'I worried that she had lost her eye' which is not in the JPN. JPN simply says あの人は大丈夫だろうか (Is that person okay?). New is faithful.

TextENGNew: That was all I could think about, going round and round in my head, and I couldn't bring myself to eat.
...That night, I couldn't sleep, and was racked by a terrible fever.
In the end, the first thing to punish me for my crime wasn't my parents, or school, or the police... it was my own body.
It was the boundary between late night and early morning.
...I woke my parents, who were fast asleep, and confessed my crime.
At first they were stunned, but given my recent rebellious behavior and my obsession with model guns, they seemed to grasp the situation quickly.
changeReason: Old says 'BB guns'; JPN says モデルガン (model guns). New correctly says 'model guns.'

TextENGNew: My mother wept quietly. My father beat me until my face was swollen.
In the end, without even waiting for dawn, I went to the police station, accompanied by my parents.
Satoko was staring at me with a dazed expression.
She had every right to look at me that way.
And I had a sin that I needed to accept the consequences of...
``......Is that... really true...?``
``Yeah...@k@vS09/01/170100966. It's true.@k@vS09/01/170100967. ...I'm sorry I kept it hidden until now...``
``...Why would you go out of your way to bring up something like that now?``
When Mion said that, Rika-chan nodded along in agreement.
``Honestly speaking,@k@vS09/03/170300493. ...my reaction is just, 'Kei-chan, what's gotten into you?'``
changeReason: Old says 'I don't understand why' which is truncated; new says 'my reaction is just, Kei-chan, what's gotten into you?' which captures Mion's actual quote from the JPN (圭ちゃん、どうしたのーー).

TextENGNew: ``...Well... yeah, I guess that's fair...``
``No, that's not what I mean.@k@vS09/03/170300495. ...I'm saying, was there really any need to go out of your way to tell us that?``

``Well...@k@vS09/01/170100970. ...Keeping secrets... that's not what friends do.@k@vS09/01/170100971. ...I think of all of you as my friends.@k@vS09/01/170100972. ...So... I just couldn't keep hiding it anymore.``
``...Keiichi, what exactly are you hoping to accomplish by telling us this?``
``I... I'm not hoping for anything in particular.@k@vS09/01/170100974. ...I just...``
``...Even if you want forgiveness, I can't give it to you.@k@vS09/05/170500173. Only the people you hurt can grant you that.@k@vS09/05/170500174. So I don't know what you expect from us.``
``Rika is right.@k@vS09/04/170400124. ...I'll admit it took courage, but... what exactly do you want us to do with this confession?``
``...I don't... want anything specific.@k@vS09/01/170100976. ...I just... friends shouldn't have to hide...``
My friends met my sudden confession with nothing but uncomfortable expressions.
I accepted it as the natural consequence of my sins.
``Hey, Kei-chan.@k@vS09/03/170300498. I've got a question for you.``
``...Is it really wrong to keep secrets from your friends?``
``The way you're framing it, being friends means having no secrets. Is that your argument?``
``...This old man doesn't like the sound of that one bit.``
changeReason: Old loses Mion's characteristic self-referral as おじさん (old man/uncle); new preserves it as 'This old man,' which is a defining character trait of Mion's speech.

TextENGNew: ``...I don't like it either.``
Mion and Rika-chan rejected my premise with absolute clarity.@k Satoko followed suit with a nod.

``What's wrong with not saying things you don't want to say?@k@vS09/03/170300503. Everyone has things they don't want to talk about—painful things, sad things, failures, things they'd rather not remember.``
``If you have to bare all of that to be called friends, then I don't want friends like that.``
``...B-But... if you don't, then...``
``...Keiichi.@k@vS09/05/170500177. Things you don't need to confess and things you must confess are not the same.@k@vS09/05/170500178. Sins must be confessed in order to seek forgiveness.``
``But a sin that has already been confessed and forgiven doesn't need to be confessed again.``
``That's right.@k@vS09/04/170400126. ...After all, Keiichi-san, you went to the police and were properly scolded, were you not?``
``If you atoned for it and were forgiven, then that's the end of your sin.``
``You turned yourself in and accepted the consequences. That means your duty is done.``
``Keeping it engraved in your heart so you never repeat the mistake—that's the right thing to do.``
``...But that's something you do privately, as a reminder to yourself. It's not something you have to confess to us.``
``Exactly.@k@vS09/04/170400129. I have plenty of embarrassing things from my past that I'd rather keep to myself.``
``...Mii.@k@vS09/05/170500181. Satoko has lots and lots and lots.``
``I-It's not just me!!@k@vS09/04/170400131. You too, Rika! You too!``
``Mii mii mii☆``
Satoko and Rika-chan played around the way they always did.
Watching them, Mion turned back to me.
``I think Satoko just said something very wise.@k@vS09/03/170300510. ...Can you tell me which part, Kei-chan?``
``...The part about wanting to keep past things secret...?``
``Nope!@k@vS09/03/170300512. The next part.``
``The part about everyone having them.@k@vS09/03/170300514. Unless you're a human raised in a sterile test tube, life is nothing but trial and error.``
``Good things and bad things—you go through all kinds of experiences as you grow. And if you end up a wonderful person at the very end, isn't that enough?``

``Good things and bad things are all precious experiences.@k@vS09/03/170300517. That's the same for everyone—not just you, Kei-chan, but me, Satoko, and Rika-chan too.@k@vS09/03/170300518. Every single one of us has things we can't be proud of.``
``...For example, Mii used to be a notorious bad kitty once upon a time.@k@vS09/05/170500184. Nyaa nyaa.``
``Heh heh heh!@k@vS09/03/170300520. ...Well, I'd rather not get into it, honestly.@k@vS09/03/170300521. Compared to what I did, ambushing people with a model gun—ahaha—is cute stuff.``

``I was throwing bricks at people, seriously.@k@vS09/03/170300523. I can't even count how many times I ended up at the police station.@k@vS09/03/170300524. During the dam war, I even spent some time in a holding cell. No joke.``
``...But that's all in the past.@k@vS09/05/170500186. And she doesn't need to tell Keiichi about it.@k@vS09/05/170500187. Even among friends.``
``Even among... friends...``
``...I, too, have made bitter mistakes and committed sins I could never speak of.@k@vS09/04/170400133. But I don't speak of them because there's nothing to be gained by it.``

``If talking about it makes you feel better, we'll listen.@k@vS09/03/170300527. But there is no rule that says friends must confess everything!@k@vS09/03/170300528. If anything, being friends means not forcing someone to say what they don't want to say.``
``...Y-Yeah... I suppose you're right, but...``

``If confessing makes you feel lighter, Kei-chan, then that's fine.@k@vS09/03/170300530. We'll forget today's talk as soon as we can.@k@vS09/03/170300531. And hearing this doesn't change what Kei-chan means to us one bit.``
``...What Keiichi did in his previous town has nothing to do with who Keiichi is now.``
``If the Keiichi of today is an irreplaceable friend to us, then I have no interest in knowing about the other town.``
``Rika is absolutely right.@k@vS09/04/170400135. Conversely, no matter how many good deeds you piled up elsewhere, if you were an unpleasant person here, those good deeds would mean nothing to us.``
``So, Kei-chan.@k@vS09/03/170300533. Keeping it close to your heart as a private reminder never to repeat the mistake—isn't that enough? You don't need to go around announcing it.``
``In fact, even if you did announce it:@k@vS09/03/170300535. If someone changed the way they saw you because of that, I'd look down on them, not you!``
``No matter what your past holds, if who you are now is admirable, what reason is there to be ashamed?``
``If past mistakes could never be overcome... then what would any of us be living for?``
``...Satoko was terribly spoiled and dependent in the past.@k@vS09/05/170500191. She regrets that, and now she works so very hard every day.``
``That is why I think the Satoko of today is truly wonderful.@k@vS09/05/170500193. I have no interest in what she used to be.``
``How about you, Kei-chan?``
``If you found out I was a repeat offender with a police record, would that change the way you see me?``
``You wouldn't, right?@k@vS09/03/170300539. I'm me.@k@vS09/03/170300540. We can still goof around and have fun together just the same, can't we?@k@vS09/03/170300541. Nothing changes.``
Huh... Huh... What...
...Mion's encouraging smile... blurred and warped...
From my eyes... large teardrops... fell...
...What... How... Why am I... crying...
``Let me say this first.@k@vS09/01/170100983. I don't know anything about Satoshi.@k@vS09/01/170100984. ...Because you all hid it from me.``
``...W-We weren't exactly hiding...``
``You hid the annual incidents from me, didn't you?``
``Oh, that was... because we didn't want Kei-chan to... um...``
``You didn't want to scare me, is that it?!@k@vS09/01/170100988. And that's reason enough to shut me out?!``
``No, that wasn't our intention...``
Mion's eyes welled up with tears as she floundered for words.
...She had been considerate for my sake as a friend, and she couldn't understand how it had come to this.
She'd wanted to make sure a newcomer like me wouldn't be frightened of the village.@k ...What more reason could anyone need?!
``I asked you directly at the dam site whether anything had happened there.@k@vS09/01/170100990. ...And you said nothing had!!!@k@vS09/01/170100992. You liar!!!``
``I-I'm sorry...!!@k@vS09/03/170300545. I wasn't exactly lying...``
``Friends don't keep secrets, right?@k@vS09/01/170100994. Right?!@k@vS09/01/170100995. Then you're not my friends!!``
AAAAAHHHHH!!!
What does 'friend' even mean?! What does 'friend' mean?! What were my friends to me?!
They had been true friends.
They had genuinely worried about me.
They had gone out of their way to help me fit into this village!!
What had I been dissatisfied with?!
changeReason: Old converts rhetorical question into positive statement 'I couldn't have been happier!' -- loses self-questioning tone.

TextENGNew: What hadn't I liked?!
changeReason: Old converts rhetorical question 'What hadn't I liked?!' into 'Everything was great' -- loses self-recrimination.

TextENGNew: ``K-Kei-chan... That's not...``
Mion looked helpless and flustered.
She even seemed on the verge of tears.@k ...It was impossible to imagine from her usual self.
I could feel Mion's pain—the pain of being attacked with words she couldn't understand the reason for...
She was looking at me with such sad eyes... How could I not notice that pain?!
And yet I had the nerve to talk about friendship!!
changeReason: Old says 'I thought I was her friend' (asserting friendship); new correctly conveys self-reproach: 'I had the nerve to talk about friendship' (そのくせ、何が仲間だ = 'and yet, what friendship!').

TextENGNew: I couldn't understand my friends' feelings! I couldn't trust them!
Brandishing the word 'friend' like a weapon... what had I been doing?!
``...That's right... I was... scum...!!@k@vS09/01/170100997. Mion was Mion—she'd always been my best friend!!@k@vS09/01/170100998. Why couldn't I trust... the best friend I ever had...?!``
changeReason: Old translates 屑 (trash/scum) as 'fool'; new correctly renders it as 'scum,' which carries the much stronger self-loathing in the JPN.

TextENGNew: ``Hey Kei-chan, you haven't been yourself lately.``
``He moved here from the city, after all.``
``The fatigue is probably just catching up with him now.@k@vS09/02/170201329. I bet that's how he caught his cold.``
``I see, I see.@k@vS09/03/170300549. Then we need to get some nutrition into him!@k@vS09/03/170300550. Gran's making ohagi today.@k@vS09/03/170300551. Let's bring some to him as a get-well gift!``
``Mion, wash your hands first!!!@k@vS09/17/172300002. Oh my, Rena-chan, would you like to help make one?``
``Oh... Making ohagi looks like fun!``
``Maybe I'll make just one too.@k@vS09/02/170201332. Heave-ho, heave-ho.``
``Heh heh heh!@k@vS09/03/170300553. I know—I just had a great idea.``
``What is it, Mii-chan?``
``...Ooh, what's that little bottle?``
``All sweet ohagi would be boring, right?``
``Heh heh heh, I'll put Tabasco sauce in one of them—critical hit style!``
That's right... That's what happened...!!!
They came to visit me when I was sick... bringing ohagi... trying to cheer me up!!
So... why was I afraid of that?!
Just because Mion somehow knew I'd been eating at Angel Mort—why was I so terrified of that?!
``...Keiichi-kun, you don't look well.@k@vS09/02/170201336. I think you should lie down.``
``She's right.@k@vS09/03/170300557. We should head home.``
They were just teasing me to lift my spirits.
But... I was taking it so seriously that they must have seen it on my face...
``See you, Kei-chan.``
Get well soon.@k Let's all play at the club together tomorrow!
Of course that's how they'd say goodbye...!!
``Don't you dare skip school tomorrow, okay?``
...Had I ever visited a classmate who was home sick with a cold?
Never, right?!
I'd never bothered to do something that inconvenient—not even once!
But... they did it for me!
It wasn't even a hospital stay—I'd just caught a little cold!
And they came all the way to visit, bringing homemade ohagi...!
I should have been grateful—I should have said I'd get better by tomorrow and come to school!!
I should have answered, see you tomorrow at school!—that's what I should have said!!
So what did I actually do?!
I locked the door!!
As if rejecting those two... I shut the door and locked it!!
What was I afraid of?! What was I scared of?!
Why couldn't I feel gratitude toward the friends who cared about me?!
Why was I so afraid of the ohagi?!
There couldn't have been... anything in them!!
changeReason: Old says 'What was I thinking?!' but Japanese means 'There couldn't have been anything in them!!' -- about the ohagi.

TextENGNew: There were never any needles in the ohagi!!
Nothing was in them—nothing at all!!
I'd just remembered some scene from a manga or something, where someone put razor blades or sewing needles in food—
...and once the thought crossed my mind that something like that might be inside... for some reason... I became convinced it was actually there...!!!
You idiot, Keiichi!!!
Why would friends put needles in food they brought to a sick friend?!
That's the most impossible thing in the world!!
Even hypothetically!@k Even if there really had been a needle, any real friend would believe it was an accident and trust in their innocence!!!@k And what did I do instead?!
SPLAT!
SPLAT!!
SPLAT!!!
One after another, with all my strength... I hurled the ohagi they'd put their hearts into making—and desecrated them!!
Why?!@k How could I?!
I had loved them—all of them.
Moving to Hinamizawa was the first time I'd ever found real friends.
They were the best companions I could have asked for.
The kind of encounter you might go your entire life without having—the very best.
I knew that—and yet... how did I end up fearing and suspecting them?!
My friends had... gradually noticed something was wrong with me.
...That's right—they called the coach for me.
They thought maybe a sedative and some rest would fix me... and they carried me to my room.
Mion had wanted to play a little prank.
Mion was the type who expressed affection through mischief—a bit of a contrarian, that was all.
I knew that perfectly well...!!
So... she anticipated that when the coach arrived, he'd lift my shirt to use a stethoscope,@k ...and she wanted to doodle on it first—the same penalty game as Tomitake-san's...!
That's right... that's why Mion... pulled out a marker.
A marker.
...She'd thought up that stupid little prank and gone out of her way to bring a marker from home.
She didn't think she was cut out for bedside nursing, so she tried to cheer me up with a penalty game instead... and she'd brought the marker specifically for that...!
All of it—just to cheer me up...!
Rena held me from behind... Mion came close... and she... what was she going to write...?
Mion... she was... mouthing the words... as she prepared to write on my shirt...!!
I... heard her.@k ...I was listening.
What Mion was going to scribble on me... it had reached my ears...!!!
Get well soon☆ —Mion
I heard it—I heard it, and yet...!!
If I could hear it... then I should have known immediately that Mion would never do anything to harm me!!!
So why—why did I—!!
How did that look like a syringe to me?!
How could I have believed Mion was trying to kill me?!
It's impossible! It's impossible!!
Why, why, why, why!!
How... how could I have made such an insane mistake... believing Mion was out for my life?!
The ringing numbness in my head cleared, and I could feel warmth slowly returning to my entire body.
How long... had I been curled up here...?@k Minutes?@k Or tens of minutes...?
...When I looked up at the clock, the hands seemed as though they had stopped completely during the time my eyes were closed—they had barely moved at all.
...Really?
The air filling the room now was not the chaos that had consumed it moments before... it held only gray silence.
Rena, who should have been restraining me, was gone. Mion, who had been bearing down on me with the injection, was gone.
changeReason: Old says 'Rena wasn't holding me down, Mion wasn't about to give me an injection'; new correctly adds that they were 'gone' (いなかった), emphasizing they had vanished from the room.

TextENGNew: ...Could it all have been... some kind of... hallucination?!
There was no trace of anyone else in the room...
An abnormal experience unlike anything I'd ever known.
...I was certain... Rena and Mion had...
I doubted my own sanity for a moment, but at the same time I felt a strange relief.
Ha ha ha... So those terrifying events really were... just a hallucination.
Rena and Mion... would never do something so terrible...
The corners of my eyes... grew hot.
I could feel emotion welling up...
Why...?
I wasn't asking why tears were coming.
Why...?
...It was... grief.
...Why was I feeling such grief...?@k I didn't understand.@k ...I didn't understand...
I didn't understand?
What don't I understand?!@k I understood perfectly!!
What was I doing—was I stupid?!
Why was I such a hopelessly unsalvageable piece of trash?!
changeReason: Old softens to 'pathetic and helpless'; new correctly translates クズ as 'trash' and preserves 救えない (unsalvageable), matching the intense self-loathing in the JPN.

TextENGNew: Mion had been trying to cheer me up until the very end!
She was dear to me, she was my best friend, she was the greatest companion I ever had!
She was older than me, but she'd never once made me feel the difference!
...She was the very first person in the class to talk to me.@k And Mion—that Mion—how could I?!
And there she was, lying in an unnatural position by the window.
...Dark, sticky red covered her from head to chest.
The red splattered across the wall... had come from the head I had battered and cracked open, again and again and again.
changeReason: Old merely says 'The red fluid... was Mion's'; new includes the graphic detail of repeated bludgeoning (俺が何度も何度も打ち付けて叩き割った頭から), significantly changing the reader's understanding of the violence.

TextENGNew: What had I done... what had I done...?!@k I had done something... irreversible...!!
``I... truly believed they were my friends...``
changeReason: Old addresses present company ('you all'); new reflects on the past ('they were my friends'), matching the JPN past tense and context of remembering another world.

TextENGNew: So how... how did it come to this...?
Nothing at my previous school had been fun.
...All I did was obsess over the rise and fall of my test scores, debating whether my first choice was in my range or my safety zone...@k ...A gray existence.
``Friends`` were just study rivals in class—enemies competing over school rankings and internal test scores.
The ones who taught me how unhealthy that life was... were them.
This past month had been truly wonderful...
We'd made a huge fuss over lunches, made a huge fuss at club activities, made a huge fuss at the festival...
Something hot began pouring from my eyes.@k ...Before I knew it... they were tears.
I had no reason to shed tears for these people.@k ...But I couldn't stop them...!!
Even if they'd been trying to kill me.
Even if I'd nearly been murdered.
...The memories of this past month... were unforgettable...
Or... had those happy days... been a lie...?
Had it all been... an elaborate trap, carefully maintained until today to ensnare me...?
Had I been the only one who thought we were friends...?
That couldn't be true...!!!!
Rena and Mion... were truly my friends!!
Not a single cloud or falsehood tainted those happy days...!!!
Rena and the others... must have been forced by someone to try to kill me.
Or perhaps... they had been possessed by the supernatural entity called Oyashiro-sama, their wills hijacked!
In any case... Rena and Mion... were the best friends I ever had!!
You IDIOT!!
Idiot, idiot, idiot, idiot!
What kind of nonsense am I spouting?!@k I beat the best friend I ever had to death with my own hands—and now I'm running my mouth about it?!
She was the greatest friend, the greatest companion!!
changeReason: Old generalizes to plural 'They were my best friends'; new correctly narrows to singular 'She was the greatest friend,' matching context where Keiichi is specifically lamenting what he did to Mion.

TextENGNew: Then why couldn't I believe her?!
changeReason: Old uses plural 'them'; new correctly uses singular 'her' (Mion), matching the passage's focus on Mion specifically.

TextENGNew: Why couldn't I listen to her words?!
changeReason: Old uses plural 'them'; new correctly uses singular 'her words,' continuing the Mion-specific focus.

TextENGNew: And after beating her to death, who do I think I am?!
changeReason: Old says 'I beat them to death' (plural); new correctly says 'beating her to death' (singular). In this arc context, Keiichi killed Mion specifically.

TextENGNew: Acting as though I bore no fault at all?!
Feeling as if everyone else in the world had gone mad?
You fool—it was the opposite!!
Of course it was the opposite!!@k The only one who'd gone mad was me!!!
``I... I... What have I done...@k@vS09/01/170101002. ...What have I... ahhhhh...``
Mion is alive, right here, right now.
...So my memory of beating Mion to death in a fit of paranoia must belong to ``another world that never was.``
But... even if it was another world... I killed Mion.
And I hadn't even been aware of the gravity of that sin.
Worse still, until this very moment, I had blithely assumed Mion had deserved what I did to her!!
``Wh-What's wrong... Keiichi-san...?``
``I don't know...@k@vS09/03/170300562. Kei-chan, calm down, calm down...@k@vS09/03/170300563. I'm alive, see?``
``Like I'd let Kei-chan beat me to death—as if!``
``Right?``
``Precisely.@k@vS09/04/170400141. No member of our club would ever fall behind against the likes of Keiichi-san!``
``No, that's not it... That's not it...``
Neither Mion nor Satoko understood why I had suddenly burst into tears, and they were bewildered.
But... all I could do was cry.
I had just learned that sins must be confessed to earn forgiveness.
...But this sin of mine... was one this Mion could never forgive...
No matter how much I cried in ``this world``... no one could forgive me.@k This sin would never, ever be absolved...
Just then, Chie-sensei arrived and noticed I was crying.
...It's not every day you see a boy my age in tears.
Chie-sensei asked what had happened, but her voice didn't reach me.
Mion and Satoko were questioned about the situation, and they seemed at a loss.
As my tears refused to stop, Rika-chan brought her face close to mine.
``...Keiichi... do you remember?@k@vS09/05/170500195. ...That you killed... Rena and Mii?``
``Yes!!@k@vS09/01/170101005. I... killed them!!@k@vS09/01/170101006. I killed them!!@k@vS09/01/170101007. And yet, I felt no remorse at all... not the slightest...!!``
``There was blood everywhere... and then... and then...@k@vS09/01/170101010. In the end, without a shred of regret... alone... I just... died...``
In a phone booth, clawing at my unbearably itchy throat,@k ...still oblivious to my own sin,@k ...dying alone.
``...Keiichi.@k@vS09/05/170500197. ...Have you... become aware of your sin?``
``I have.@k@vS09/01/170101012. Only now, this late—I finally realized it, remembered it!!@k@vS09/01/170101013. In another world, very much like this one!@k@vS09/01/170101014. I killed my friends!``

``My friends must have been calling out to me with words of salvation!@k@vS09/01/170101016. Why couldn't I hear them?@k@vS09/01/170101017. Why?!@k@vS09/01/170101018. Why couldn't I have trusted them?!``
I no longer cared who was watching.
...I simply wept.@k I wanted to wring my sins out through my tears.
But what came out wasn't sin.
...Only tears of remorse for something irretrievable and irreversible.
Then.@k ...A touch on my head. A gentle stroking.
...When I opened my eyes, Rika-chan was patting my head with the palm of her hand.
...I didn't need comfort.
There was no way Rika-chan could comfort me.
But Rika-chan spoke.
...This was not comfort.
``...I shall forgive Keiichi.``
changeReason: Old has Rika say 'I forgive you' in direct address; new says 'I shall forgive Keiichi' preserving the third-person reference (圭一を、許しましょう) characteristic of Rika's speech pattern.

TextENGNew: ``...Keiichi became aware of his own sin, all by himself.@k@vS09/05/170500200. That is... very admirable, and... something that should not be possible.``
``And so no one else can understand how remarkable that is, and no one else can grant you forgiveness for it.@k@vS09/05/170500202. ...But I can.@k@vS09/05/170500203. I will forgive you.``
``...Rika-chan...``

``...I know that this is a miracle.@k@vS09/05/170500205. Because it should be impossible.@k@vS09/05/170500206. That you... could become aware of your other self's sin... is nothing short of miraculous.``
``It is something that would eclipse any miracle ever made manifest upon this earth... something unbelievably precious.``
I couldn't begin to understand what Rika-chan was saying.
...And yet... I felt that Rika-chan alone could understand this impossible memory of mine.
``...Then.@k@vS09/05/170500210. You, as you are now, should be able to understand.@k@vS09/05/170500211. ...What is happening to Ryuugu Rena.``

``Yeah... I understand...@k@vS09/01/170101022. She's going through the same thing I did...!!@k@vS09/01/170101023. She wants so desperately to believe in her friends, but she can't!!@k@vS09/01/170101024. All she can do is keep doubting and doubting!``

``She keeps getting more and more deranged, but she can't see it—so she's convinced it's the world that's gone mad!!@k@vS09/01/170101026. I don't know how to save her!!@k@vS09/01/170101027. Because I couldn't even save myself!``

``Because... I failed!!@k@vS09/01/170101029. In the end, I never realized—not until I died!@k@vS09/01/170101030. No—not even after dying!!@k@vS09/01/170101031. If I failed, then how could I possibly save Rena?!``
``I know because I went through the same thing...@k@vS09/01/170101034. No matter what you do, it's impossible to save them...``
...Calm down, Keiichi...
Sure, I was beyond saving.
...But that doesn't necessarily mean Rena is too...?
Just because it seems hopeless doesn't mean you can give up on saving her.
...What about Rena?
Even when I'd deteriorated to that terminal state of madness... she threw herself bodily at me, trying to save me with everything she had!!
In my room, in that other world... beaten again and again with a metal bat...!
And still, Rena smiled.
She knew I was terrified... so she smiled, to keep me from being afraid.
And even after I'd struck her over and over, even with her arms mangled and her forehead split open...
That's right... at the very, very end.@k ...Rena stopped shielding her head.
Like this... both hands reaching out... searching for someone to embrace... like this...
And still unable to trust Rena, I... raised the metal bat high.
Rena spoke.
It's okay.
``...Believe in me.``
AAAAHHHHH!!
My friend told me to believe!!@k She told me to believe in her!
How could I not believe her?! I was the one who'd lost his mind and was attacking her!!
changeReason: Old says 'I still didn't [believe]. I lost it and attacked her'; new correctly captures the self-questioning 'How could I not believe her?!' and the recognition that Keiichi was the deranged one, matching JPN.

TextENGNew: Her life was in danger—and in fact, she was killed!!
And yet, instead of shielding her head, she held out both hands... reaching for me...!
Rena kept saying those words until the moment she died.
Believe in me.@k Please—believe in me.
She had staked her life on saving me—on shaking me awake!!
Until the very moment her life was taken... she kept saying ``believe``...
And I couldn't hear my friend's words, and until this very moment, I had honestly believed I was blameless!!!

``...Having Rika-chan forgive me lightened my heart a little.@k@vS09/01/170101036. ...But my sin still has to be destroyed in the same form it took.@k@vS09/01/170101037. I have to repay Rena what she gave.``
Rena staked her life!!
She worried about me until the moment she died!
She believed!!
She believed that the next words would be the ones to reach me, and she said them over and over!!@k Until the very last moment when her skull was split open!!
My life is nothing to mourn...
Rena proved it by dying!!
I can offer my life just as easily!!
I was a worthless fool who couldn't understand his friends' feelings, and that's why their words never reached me.
...But Rena... Rena might be able to reach her...!!
``...This world has already gone mad.@k@vS09/01/170101039. But that doesn't mean we should give up.@k@vS09/01/170101040. ...Giving up means it's truly over!``
``I'll believe in Rena—in the bond between friends.@k@vS09/01/170101042. ...It's not too late.@k@vS09/01/170101043. The tragedy can still be averted.@k@vS09/01/170101044. I'll prove it!``
Rika-chan looked down solemnly, her fingers toying with something in her skirt pocket.
changeReason: Old says Rika 'nodded'; JPN says 俯く (looked down). Also old says 'put her hand in the pocket' while new more accurately says 'fingers toying with something,' matching いじるような仕草.

TextENGNew: ``...Keiichi.@k@vS09/05/170500214. ...I, too... will not give up.``
``...I refuse to lose hope in this world.@k@vS09/05/170500216. ...I was exhausted from countless failures.@k@vS09/05/170500217. So I had resigned myself to things going wrong.``
``That's why... last night, I told myself I didn't care what happened to Rena anymore.@k@vS09/05/170500219. But... that was wrong.``
``...The will to fight is so beautiful, so sacred... and it can work miracles that bend even fate itself.``

``So I will fight alongside you.@k@vS09/05/170500222. I will fight once more,@k@vS09/05/170500223. I will fight as many times as it takes.@k@vS09/05/170500224. Until I find the future that waits beyond.``
``...I... I haven't the faintest idea what you and Keiichi-san are talking about.``
``Hee hee...@k@vS09/05/170500226. ...It's only natural that you don't remember.@k@vS09/05/170500227. ...Keiichi is simply... extraordinary.``
``???@k@vS09/04/170400144. My superior intellect simply cannot follow this conversation.``
Rika-chan gave Satoko a gentle headbutt.
``...But someday, please remember.@k@vS09/05/170500229. That when you were trapped in an inescapable cul-de-sac of misfortune... Keiichi fought with everything he had to save you.``
``Keiichi-san... did that?@k@vS09/04/170400146. That sounds rather dubious, I must say...``
``Hee hee.@k@vS09/05/170500231. ...Right, Keiichi?``
Rika-chan looked at me and smiled, as if seeking my agreement.
...I wasn't sure if I'd stake my life for Satoko specifically, but...
...No—I would.
Satoko is my friend.
If an enemy appeared that tried to plunge Satoko into the depths of misery, I would crush that enemy without mercy.
With everything I had!
...There might have been... a world like that, too...
So I nodded back to Rika-chan.
Rika-chan saw it and nodded happily in return.
``...Maebara-kun... did something happen...?``
The teacher must have thought I'd finally calmed down, because she came over to ask what was wrong.
``No... it's nothing.@k@vS09/01/170101048. I'm sorry for the disturbance.@k@vS09/01/170101049. ...Anyway... Mion.``
I walked straight up to Mion.
Not understanding what was happening, Mion backed away until she bumped into the blackboard.
``Wh-What is it, Kei-chan...?@k@vS09/03/170300569. ...Why the serious face...?``
I embraced Mion.
She had no idea what was happening, and could only stand there, face burning red, at a complete loss for words.
On Mion's shoulder... I spoke through my tears.
``...I'm sorry... I'm truly sorry...``
``Wh-wh-what for, Kei-chan... Um, uh, I really don't know what you're talking about...``
``...You don't have to understand... It's fine if you don't...@k@vS09/01/170101052. ...You don't have to understand... just listen...``
``...I'm truly sorry... And... the ohagi...@k@vS09/01/170101054. ...They were delicious... They were really, truly delicious...@k@vS09/01/170101055. They gave me strength.``

``...So... I should have gone back to school the next day... and said, see you tomorrow at school... I couldn't say that...@k@vS09/01/170101057. ...I'm sorry... for taking so long to say... thank you...``
``I will never—never again doubt Mion, or any of my friends!!@k@vS09/01/170101059. Never!!@k@vS09/01/170101060. Never again, absolutely!!@k@vS09/01/170101061. I swear it!``
changeReason: Old includes Keiichi's plea 'So Mion... please forgive me for what I did that day' (matching JPN どうか許してくれ); new replaces this with 'I swear it!' — omitting the crucial forgiveness plea.

TextENGNew: Mion couldn't have understood what I was talking about.
Of course... even I...
...was starting to lose track of what I was saying.
But I bore an unabsolved sin.
So I had to confess it and beg for forgiveness.
``I know what Mion risked for me...@k@vS09/01/170101063. So... if the day ever comes when misfortune finds you, I will stake my life to save you.@k@vS09/01/170101064. I swear it.``
``K-Kei-chan...?@k@vS09/03/170300573. Ha, ha ha, this is... embarrassing...``
Mion, blushing and bewildered, had no idea what was happening.
Satoko was equally red-faced, question marks written plainly across her expression.
But Rika-chan and I alone understood everything.
I refuse to accept the tragedy...!!
There's no such thing as a point of no return once the world begins to change!
I will defy fate until the very last moment!
Mion fought back too.@k Rena fought back too.
So... I will save Rena.
Just as she did for me... I'll stake my life on it.
